mnemonics.title: Three Schema Levels
mnemonics.content: ECI - External Conceptual Internal. Remember: 'Everyone Can Learn Internally' - starts from External (user view) to Internal (storage).

mnemonics.title: Data Model Types
mnemonics.content: HNRO - Hierarchical, Network, Relational, Object. Remember as 'Has No Real Order' - though relational is most common today.

mnemonics.title: Schema vs Instance
mnemonics.content: S-I - Schema is Static (designed once), Instance is Instantaneous (changes constantly).

mnemonics.title: Data Independence Types
mnemonics.content: LAP - Logical Abstracts from External, Physical Abstracts from Conceptual. Remember: 'Logical And Physical' independence.

acronyms.usage: Software system that enables creation, maintenance, and manipulation of databases
Organization that standardized the network data model
Language used to define database schemas
Language used to manipulate data in the database
Language used for access control and security

visualTips.visualization: Draw three concentric circles: Inner circle = Internal Schema (storage), Middle circle = Conceptual Schema (logical), Outer circle = External Schema (user views). Arrows between them show independence.
Think of a form (schema) vs filled forms (instances). The form template stays the same while filled content changes.
Use a table: Hierarchical = Tree, Network = Graph, Relational = Table, Object-Oriented = Class diagram.
Stack diagram: External (top) → Conceptual (middle) → Internal (bottom). Changes below affect above only, not vice versa.

analogies.analogy: Like a blueprint (schema) of a building vs the actual built building (instance). The blueprint is designed once, while the building can be modified.
Like different views of a city: Tourist map (External), City planning map (Conceptual), Underground utility maps (Internal) - all describing the same city differently.
Like changing the engine of a car (internal) without affecting the driver's view (external) or car design (conceptual).
Like an organizational chart - each employee reports to one manager, but a manager may have multiple subordinates.
Like a social network graph where people can have multiple connections to many others - not restricted to single parent.
